commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: Needs contributor live proof and maintainer policy sign-off; there is no narrow automated repair because the missing evidence must come from a real setup.; Cleared: No supply-chain, dependency, workflow, secret-handling, or permission-broadening regression is visible in the diff; remaining security work is policy and proof validation. (sha=433bc658436c64870c1a2680f859e049dfbaf5e3)
commands.review_summary:
Adds Convex package sanction batches, package soft-delete reason metadata, user ban/delete/unban hooks, regression tests, changelog, and moderation spec updates for package/plugin resources owned by sanctioned personal accounts.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level. Current main's user ban/delete paths do not touch package rows or package publish tokens, while the PR adds explicit package batches and focused regression coverage.
commands.review_followup: Needs contributor live proof and maintainer policy sign-off; there is no narrow automated repair because the missing evidence must come from a real setup.

target.title: fix: hide package resources when owners are banned
target.body:
## Summary

Makes package/plugin resources follow personal account sanctions without crossing org ownership boundaries.

## What changed

- Personal/no-publisher packages and releases are hidden when their owner is banned or deletes their account.
- Package publish tokens for those personal rows are revoked during ban/account deletion.
- `packages.softDeletedReason` distinguishes ban-hidden packages from account-deleted packages.
- Unban restore only restores packages hidden by the matching ban.
- Org-owned packages are preserved even when a banned user remains in a legacy `ownerUserId` field.

## Public behavior

Personal package resources become unavailable when the owning account is banned or deleted. Ban-hidden personal package resources are restored only by the matching unban batch.

Org-owned package resources remain owned by the org and are not hidden just because one member, or a legacy `ownerUserId`, is sanctioned.

Policy note for maintainer review: this PR intentionally treats personal package resources as sanction-bound while preserving org-owned resources.

## Behavior proof

Focused terminal proof from owned package sanction and restore batches:

```text
$ vitest run convex/packages.public.test.ts --reporter verbose --testNamePattern "soft-deletes owned packages with a ban reason|stops stale package ban pages|retimestamps earlier ban-hidden packages|does not hide org-owned packages|restores only packages that were hidden by the matching ban batch|stops stale package restore batches|marks account-deleted packages separately|does not delete org-owned packages|does not let package owners directly restore packages hidden by a user ban"

✓ convex/packages.public.test.ts > owned package sanction batches > soft-deletes owned packages with a ban reason and revokes package publish tokens
✓ convex/packages.public.test.ts > owned package sanction batches > stops stale package ban pages when the owner has already been unbanned
✓ convex/packages.public.test.ts > owned package sanction batches > retimestamps earlier ban-hidden packages during a later ban
✓ convex/packages.public.test.ts > owned package sanction batches > does not hide org-owned packages when banning a member in the legacy owner field
✓ convex/packages.public.test.ts > owned package sanction batches > restores only packages that were hidden by the matching ban batch
✓ convex/packages.public.test.ts > owned package sanction batches > stops stale package restore batches when the owner is banned again
✓ convex/packages.public.test.ts > owned package sanction batches > marks account-deleted packages separately from ban-restorable packages
✓ convex/packages.public.test.ts > owned package sanction batches > does not delete org-owned packages when deleting a member account
✓ convex/packages.public.test.ts > restorePackageInternal > does not let package owners directly restore packages hidden by a user ban

Test Files  1 passed (1)
Tests  9 passed | 129 skipped (138)
```

Full focused suite also passed:

```text
$ bun run test convex/packages.public.test.ts
Test Files  1 passed (1)
Tests       138 passed (138)
```

## Validation

```text
$ bun run ci:static
passed
```

```text
$ bun run ci:unit
Test Files  204 passed (204)
Tests       1994 passed (1994)
Statements  85.98%
Branches    75.17%
Functions   86.37%
Lines        89.48%
```

```text
$ bun run ci:types-build
tsc, package typechecks, clawhub-mod typecheck, Vite build, and Nitro build passed.
```
target.author: vyctorbrzezowski

commands.reason: PR is not opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
